studyName: Comparison Across Multiple Types of Sleep Deprivation

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table reports numerous metrics from each subject's neurobehavioral test timepoints including visual analog scale responses, TRACK assay, STROOP assay with all four colors or selected colors, PEERS ratings, matrix reasoning tasks,  Karolinska Sleepiness Scale measurements, Digit Symbol Substitution Test (DSST) trials, and Balloon Analog Risk Test (BART) trials.

variables.name: BART_Adj_Mean_Pmps
variables.description: Bart adjusted mean pumps for successful trials that ended with collect rather than popping the balloon in the Balloon Analog Risk Test (BART)

variables.name: BART_Risk_score
variables.description: BART Risk Score calculated as (sum of TRIAL_PUMP) / (sum  of (BURST_AT - 1)), where Trial_Pump are the pumps attempted and BURST_AT is when the balloon will burst in the Balloon Analog Risk Test (BART)

variables.name: CONDITION_GROUP
variables.description: Study condition group

variables.name: CUMULATIVE_WAKE_HOURS
variables.description: Cumulative hours the subject has been awake at the time of sample collection, i.e., cumulative hours of wakefulness since the last time in bed sleep episdoe.

variables.name: CVS_RT0divbyCorr0
variables.description: An adjusted response time, calculated as RT_0 / CORRTR_0, i.e. the average reaction time of copy trials divided by the number of correct copy trials on the Comparative Visual Search (CVS) assay.

variables.name: CVS_RT1divbyCorr1
variables.description: An adjusted response time, calculated as RT_1 / CORRTR_1, i.e. the average reaction time of mirror trials divided by the number of correct mirror trials on the Comparative Visual Search (CVS) assay.

variables.name: DSST_CORRECT
variables.description: Total number of correct responses on the Digit Symbol Substitution Test (DSST).

variables.name: DSST_PERCENT
variables.description: Percentage of correct responses on the Digit Symbol Substitution Test (DSST), i.e., the number of correct responses divided by the total number of responses, expressed as a percentage

variables.name: Face_RECALL_FACES_CORR
variables.description: This is the number of correct faces on the FACE assay

variables.name: Face_RECALL_PERCENTCOR
variables.description: This is the percentage of correct faces on the FACE assay

variables.name: HOURS_INTO_STUDY
variables.description: Cumulative number of hours since midnight Relative Clock Hour at the outset of study day 3

variables.name: KSS_After_SCALE
variables.description: This is the final self rating of the Karolinska Sleepiness Scale. Scale values range from 1-9, with anchor words for 1 (extremely alert), 3 (alert), 5 (neithere alert nor sleepy), 7 (sleepy - but no difficulty remaining awake), and 9 (extremely sleepy - fighting sleep).

variables.name: KSS_Before_SCALE
variables.description: This is the initial self rating of the Karolinska Sleepiness Scale. Scale values range from 1-9, with anchor words for 1 (extremely alert), 3 (alert), 5 (neithere alert nor sleepy), 7 (sleepy - but no difficulty remaining awake), and 9 (extremely sleepy - fighting sleep).

variables.name: MRT_Sum_ADV_COR
variables.description: This is the overall number of correct advanced trials in the matrix reasoning task

variables.name: MRT_Sum_BEGIN_COR
variables.description: This is the overall number of correct beginner trials in the matrix reasoning task

variables.name: MRT_Sum_CORRECT
variables.description: This is the overall number of correct answers in the matrix reasoning task

variables.name: MRT_Sum_EXP_COR
variables.description: This is the overall number of correct expert trials in the matrix reasoning task

variables.name: MRT_Sum_INT_COR
variables.description: This is the overall number of correct intermediate trials in the matrix reasoning task

variables.name: NTB_TIMEPOINT
variables.description: Timepoint for neurobehavioral test battery

variables.name: PEERSCL_BETTER
variables.description: This is a self-rating of performing better on the second PEERS session, on a scale from 0-100. A score of 100 on PEERS scales means: Extremely Good (for PERFORM), An Extreme Effort (for EFFORT), and Yes I Could Have Done Much Better (for BETTER).

variables.name: PEERSCL_EFFORT
variables.description: This is a self-rating of performance effort on the second PEERS session, on a scale from 0-100. A score of 100 on PEERS scales means: Extremely Good (for PERFORM), An Extreme Effort (for EFFORT), and Yes I Could Have Done Much Better (for BETTER).

variables.name: PEERSCL_PERFORM
variables.description: This is a self-rating of performance on the second PEERS session, on a scale from 0-100. A score of 100 on PEERS scales means: Extremely Good (for PERFORM), An Extreme Effort (for EFFORT), and Yes I Could Have Done Much Better (for BETTER).

variables.name: P_PEERSCL_EFFORT
variables.description: This is a self-rating of performance effort on the first or Pre PEERS session, on a scale from 0-100. A score of 100 on PEERS scales means: Extremely Good (for PERFORM), An Extreme Effort (for EFFORT), and Yes I Could Have Done Much Better (for BETTER).

variables.name: P_PEERSCL_PERFORM
variables.description: This is a self-rating of performance on the first or Pre PEERS session, on a scale from 0-100. A score of 100 on PEERS scales means: Extremely Good (for PERFORM), An Extreme Effort (for EFFORT), and Yes I Could Have Done Much Better (for BETTER).

variables.name: RELATIVE_CLOCK_HOUR
variables.description: Time of sample collection expressed as Relative Clock Hours

variables.name: SCWORD_BLUEYELLOW_FAC_M_RT
variables.description: This is the mean reaction time for facilitated trials on trials with only blue and yellow colors.

variables.name: SCWORD_BLUEYELLOW_FAC_PERCCR
variables.description: This is the percent correct on facilitated trials on trials with only blue and yellow colors.

variables.name: SCWORD_BLUEYELLOW_INH_M_RT
variables.description: This is the mean reaction time for inhibited trials on trials with only blue and yellow colors.

variables.name: SCWORD_BLUEYELLOW_INH_PERCCR
variables.description: This is the percent correct on inhibited trials on trials with only blue and yellow colors.

variables.name: SCWORD_BLUEYELLOW_NEU_M_RT
variables.description: This is the mean reaction time for neutral trials on trials with only blue and yellow colors.

variables.name: SCWORD_BLUEYELLOW_NEU_PERCCR
variables.description: This is the percent correct on neutral trials on trials with only blue and yellow colors.

variables.name: SCWORD_BLUEYELLOW_OV_CORRECT
variables.description: This is the integer count of the overall number of correct responses on trials with only blue and yellow colors.

variables.name: SCWORD_RESCUE_FAC_M_RT
variables.description: Mean reaction time for facilitated trials for the STROOP assay with all four colors

variables.name: SCWORD_RESCUE_FAC_PERCCR
variables.description: Percent correct on facilitated trials for the STROOP assay with all four colors

variables.name: SCWORD_RESCUE_INH_M_RT
variables.description: Mean reaction time for inhibited trials for the STROOP assay with all four colors

variables.name: SCWORD_RESCUE_INH_PERCCR
variables.description: Percent correct on inhibited trials for the STROOP assay with all four colors

variables.name: SCWORD_RESCUE_NEU_M_RT
variables.description: Mean reaction time for neutral trials for the STROOP assay with all four colors

variables.name: SCWORD_RESCUE_NEU_PERCCR
variables.description: Percent correct on neutral trials for the STROOP assay with all four colors

variables.name: SCWORD_RESCUE_OV_CORRECT
variables.description: Integer count of the overall number of correct responses for the STROOP assay with all four colors

variables.name: STUDY_DAY
variables.description: Day in the study when sample was collected

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_NTB_TIMEPOINT
variables.description: Subject and neurobehavioral test battery timepoint

variables.name: TRKALL_TOTAL_LOSS
variables.description: Count of the sum total of right and left losses in the unstable TRACK assay

variables.name: VAS_ALERT
variables.description: Visual analog scale  response from 0 to 100, 100 being more Alert. The scale is presented as a 100-mm bar that the participant marks with a cursor. The score is determined as the distance from the anchor word to the cursor.

variables.name: VAS_CALM
variables.description: Visual analog scale  response from 0 to 100, 100 being more CALM. The scale is presented as a 100-mm bar that the participant marks with a cursor. The score is determined as the distance from the anchor word to the cursor.

variables.name: VAS_CLEARHEADE
variables.description: Visual analog scale  response from 0 to 100, 100 being more Clear Headed. The scale is presented as a 100-mm bar that the participant marks with a cursor. The score is determined as the distance from the anchor word to the cursor.

variables.name: VAS_SAD
variables.description: Visual analog scale  response from 0 to 100, 100 being more Sad. The scale is presented as a 100-mm bar that the participant marks with a cursor. The score is determined as the distance from the anchor word to the cursor.

variables.name: VAS_UNMOTIVATE
variables.description: Visual analog scale response from 0 to 100, 100 being more Unmotivated. The scale is presented as a 100-mm bar that the participant marks with a cursor. The score is determined as the distance from the anchor word to the cursor.

variables.name: VPVTALL_IALL_MEAN
variables.description: Mean average of 1000/RT, where RT is the reaction time on the psychomotor vigilance test.

variables.name: VPVTALL_LAPSES
variables.description: Count of the number of times a response was greater than 500 milliseconds during the session

variables.name: VPVTALL_LAPSE_TRAN
variables.description: Transformation of psychomotor vigilance lapses calculated as LAPSE_TRAN = SQRT(LAPSES) + SQRT(LAPSES + 1)

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table reports several metrics from each subject's sleep studies.

variables.name: CONDITION_GROUP
variables.description: Study condition group

variables.name: CONDITION_GROUP_SEGMENT
variables.description: Condition group and polysomnography (PSG) segment

variables.name: LAST_EPOCH_SLEEP
variables.description: Epoch number of last epoch scored as sleep, based on 30 second epochs

variables.name: LIGHTS_ON_EPOCH
variables.description: Lights On Epoch Number, based on 30 second epochs

variables.name: LIGHTS_OUT_EPOCH
variables.description: Lights Out Epoch Number, based on 30 second epochs

variables.name: MVT
variables.description: Movement Time, minutes

variables.name: MVT_Percent
variables.description: Movement Time, percent of SPD

variables.name: REM
variables.description: REM, minutes

variables.name: REM_PERCENT
variables.description: REM, percent of SPD

variables.name: SEGMENT
variables.description: Segment in sleep study, indication of baseline, first recovery, or second recovery night

variables.name: SLEEP_EFFICIENCY
variables.description: Percentage=[(TST-(Wake+MVT+Undefined))/TST]

variables.name: SLEEP_LATENCY
variables.description: Sleep onset defined as first three consecutive epochs of sleep, time since LightsOut in minutes

variables.name: SLEEP_PERIOD_NUMBER
variables.description: Sleep period number as it occurs in protocol, with numbers indicating the consecutive counts of inpatient time in bed sleep opportunities

variables.name: SPD
variables.description: Sleep Period Duration, minutes, (LightsOn_Epoch-LightsOutEpoch+1)/2

variables.name: SPT
variables.description: Sleep Period Time, minutes, = SPD - Sleep_Latency - WASF

variables.name: STAGE1
variables.description: Sleep scored Stage N1, minutes

variables.name: STAGE1_Percent
variables.description: Sleep scored Stage N1, percent of SPD

variables.name: STAGE2
variables.description: Sleep scored Stage N2, minutes

variables.name: STAGE2_Percent
variables.description: Sleep scored Stage N2, percent of SPD

variables.name: STAGE3
variables.description: Sleep scored Stage N3 (Slow Wave Sleep), minutes

variables.name: STAGE3_Percent
variables.description: Sleep scored Stage N3 (Slow Wave Sleep), percent of SPD

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_SEGMENT
variables.description: Subject and polysomnography (PSG) segment

variables.name: TST
variables.description: Total Sleep Time within sleep onset to sleep offset, minutes

variables.name: UNDEFINED
variables.description: Undefined, minutes

variables.name: UNDEFINED_Percent
variables.description: Undefines, percent of SPD

variables.name: WAKE
variables.description: Wake, minutes

variables.name: WAKE_PERCENT
variables.description: Wake, percent of SPD

variables.name: WASF
variables.description: Wake after Sleep Offset, minutes, =(LightsOut_Epoch - Last_Epoch_Sleep)/2

variables.name: WASO
variables.description: Wake After Sleep Onset, minutes

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table reports several metrics from each subject's self assessment of their sleep quality.

variables.name: ANXIOUS
variables.description: Response from requesting subject to mark a line at a point which most accurately represents how anxious the subject feels at this very moment, based on a 100mm visual analog scale anchored with words at 0 ofExtremely calm and at 100 of Extremely agitated.

variables.name: COLOR_D
variables.description: Response to question:  Did you dream in color last night?

variables.name: CONDITION_GROUP
variables.description: Study condition group

variables.name: DREAMT
variables.description: Response to question:  How much do you think you dreamt last night (independent of how much you remember)?

variables.name: EMOTION_D
variables.description: Response to question:  How emotional were your dreams last night?

variables.name: HOSTIL_D
variables.description: Response to question:  How much violence or hostility was contained in your dreams last night?

variables.name: INFL_Q_SLP
variables.description: Response to question:  How much do you feel your experiences during the two hours or so prior to the time you went to bed influenced the quality of your sleep?

variables.name: NAWAKEN
variables.description: Response to question:  How many times did you wake up during the night?

variables.name: N_DREAMS
variables.description: Response to question:  How many different dreams can you specifically remember having?

variables.name: PHY_ACT_D
variables.description: Response to question:  How much physical activity was there in your dreams last night?

variables.name: PLEASANT_D
variables.description: Response to question:  How pleasant were your dreams last night?

variables.name: REFRES_NOW
variables.description: Response to question:  How refreshed do you feel right now?

variables.name: SESSION
variables.description: Consecutive count indicating the corresponding sleep period number

variables.name: SLEEPY_NOW
variables.description: Response to question:  How sleepy do you feel right now?

variables.name: SLPLENGTH
variables.description: Sum total of SLPLENHRS plus SLPLENMINS

variables.name: SLPLENHRS
variables.description: Response to question:  How much sleep do you think you got last night?

variables.name: SLPLENMINS
variables.description: Response to question:  How much sleep do you think you got last night?

variables.name: SLP_QUALIT
variables.description: Response to question:  How would you evaluate your sleep last night?

variables.name: SLP_SOUND
variables.description: Response to question:  How sound do you think your sleep was last night?

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_SLEEP_ESTIMATE_SEGMENT
variables.description: Subject and sleep estimate segment

variables.name: TECH_WAKUP
variables.description: Response to question:  Did your final awakening in the morning occur when the Laboratory personnel came in to awaken you?

variables.name: TENSE_NOW
variables.description: Response to question:  How tense do you feel right now?

variables.name: THINK_SLP
variables.description: Response to question:  Apart from dreaming, were you thinking in your sleep?

variables.name: VIVID_D
variables.description: Response to question:  How vivid were your dreams last night?

tables.description: This sample attributes data table reports each sample's body site of origin, analyte type, tumor status, facility performing RNA extraction on sample, cumulative study hours since midnight starting on day 3, relative clock hour for time of sample collection, collection day, subject's cumulative hours of wakefulness since sample collection, and study condition group.

variables.name: ANALYTE_TYPE
variables.description: Analyte type

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: CONDITION_GROUP
variables.description: Study condition group

variables.name: CUMULATIVE_WAKE_HOURS
variables.description: Cumulative hours the subject has been awake at the time of sample collection, i.e., cumulative hours of wakefulness since the last time in bed sleep episode.

variables.name: EXTRACTED
variables.description: Facility performing RNA extraction on sample

variables.name: HOURS_INTO_STUDY
variables.description: Cumulative number of hours since midnight Relative Clock Hour at the outset of study day 3

variables.name: IS_TUMOR
variables.description: Tumor status

variables.name: RELATIVE_CLOCK_HOUR
variables.description: Time of sample collection expressed as Relative Clock Hours

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: STUDY_DAY
variables.description: Day in the study when sample was collected

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table reports each subject's alternate subject ID, study condition group, age, sex, race, ethnicity, height, and weight.

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: Subject's age in years

variables.name: ALTERNATE_SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Alternate subject code

variables.name: CONDITION_GROUP
variables.description: Subject's study condition group

variables.name: ETHNICITY
variables.description: Subject's self-reported ethnicity

variables.name: HEIGHT
variables.description: Subject's height

variables.name: RACE
variables.description: Subject's self-reported race

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Subject's sex

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: WEIGHT
variables.description: Subject's weight

tables.description: This subject consent data table reports each subject's subject ID, consent, and biological sex.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Registered consent groups (Data Use Limitations (DUL)) as determined by submitters' Institutional Review Boards (IRB) or equivalent body.

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Biological sex

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to individual sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.

variables.name: SAMPLE_ID
variables.description: Sample ID

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID
